question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByAlias(byte[] alias)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the native SQL in findByAlias() does not use LIMIT to send an alias value that matches a large number of entity rows (if the alias column is not unique-constrained), causing the query to return a massive result set that exhausts JVM heap on ≥30% of nodes?
